description: STFU v2.0 - Source Tree Forensics & Unification: comprehensive code forensics engine with depcruise-level dependency analysis, semantic AI comparison, middleware/handler detection, shared library identification, environment consolidation, and merge wizard

userStories.title: Deep Dependency Analysis Engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want per-project dependency analysis that extracts and cross-references all dependencies from package.json (deps + devDeps), mix.exs, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, go.mod so STFU can compute precise overlap matrices.
acceptanceCriteria: New lib/stfu_core.py with DependencyAnalyzer class
Extracts deps from: package.json (dependencies + devDependencies), mix.exs ({:dep, vsn}), requirements.txt, pyproject.toml [project.dependencies], Cargo.toml [dependencies], go.mod (require block)
Builds per-project dep manifest: {name, version, category(prod/dev), ecosystem(npm/hex/pip/cargo/go)}
Computes pairwise dependency overlap matrix: shared_deps, jaccard_similarity, unique_to_a, unique_to_b
Identifies dependency clusters: groups of 3+ projects sharing >60% deps
Detects version conflicts: same dep at different versions across projects
Outputs JSON: {projects[], overlap_matrix[], clusters[], version_conflicts[]}
lfg stfu deps runs standalone dependency analysis
Typecheck passes (mypy --ignore-missing-imports lib/stfu_core.py)
userStories.notes: Foundation for all other STFU analyses. Must handle missing files gracefully.

userStories.title: File Pattern Fingerprinting
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU to detect projects built from the same template/boilerplate by fingerprinting directory structure and config patterns, so I can identify clones that should be consolidated.
acceptanceCriteria: Add FileFingerprinter class to stfu_core.py
Generates structural fingerprint per project: sorted list of relative paths (excluding node_modules, .git, dist, _build, target, __pycache__, venv)
Computes structural similarity using jaccard index on file path sets
Detects boilerplate markers: package.json 'name' field matching known templates (vite_react_shadcn_ts, next-supabase-saas-kit-turbo-lite, etc.)
Extracts config fingerprint: tsconfig.json compilerOptions hash, tailwind.config content hash, .eslintrc hash
Groups projects by template origin with confidence score
Adds 'template_groups' and 'structural_similarity' to STFU JSON output
Tests: 3+ unit tests for fingerprinting
userStories.notes: Key insight from analysis: 4 projects share vite_react_shadcn_ts template name.

userStories.title: Middleware and Handler Duplicate Detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU to find duplicate middleware, route handlers, and utility implementations across projects so I can extract shared libraries.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CodePatternDetector class to stfu_core.py
Scans for middleware files: **/middleware.ts, **/middleware.js, lib/**/plug*.ex, **/middleware.py
Scans for handler/route files: **/route.ts, **/routes/*.ts, lib/**/*_controller.ex, **/router.ex, **/views.py, **/routes.py
Computes file content similarity using SequenceMatcher (difflib) on normalized source (strip comments, normalize whitespace)
Flags pairs with >70% content similarity as 'duplicate implementations'
Extracts shared patterns: auth middleware, error handlers, CORS config, validation schemas
Categorizes findings: {auth[], error_handling[], cors[], validation[], custom[]}
Adds 'code_duplicates' section to STFU JSON output with file paths, similarity score, pattern category
Tests: 2+ unit tests
userStories.notes: Analysis found lcc/middleware.ts and lcc-major-changes/middleware.ts are 100% identical (165 LOC).

userStories.title: Shared Library Candidate Identification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU to recommend which code should be extracted into shared libraries based on duplication patterns and dependency overlap.
acceptanceCriteria: Add LibraryCandidate class to stfu_core.py
Analyzes dependency clusters + code duplicates to produce library recommendations
Generates candidates: {name, type(ui-components|auth|api-client|middleware|testing-config|build-config), source_projects[], shared_deps[], estimated_dedup_savings_mb, effort_hours, priority(1-5)}
Detects UI component library opportunity: projects sharing >10 @radix-ui deps
Detects auth library opportunity: projects with similar middleware.ts patterns
Detects testing config opportunity: projects sharing vitest/playwright/eslint configs
Adds 'library_candidates' to STFU JSON output
Tests: 2+ unit tests
userStories.notes: Analysis found potential @jeremiah/ui-core, auth-core, api-client libraries.

userStories.title: Environment Consolidation Scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU to identify projects that could share a single node_modules/venv/deps environment, scoring each consolidation opportunity by compatibility and disk savings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add EnvironmentAnalyzer class to stfu_core.py
For npm projects: compare package.json dep versions, identify groups with compatible (non-conflicting) version ranges
For Python projects: compare requirements versions for compatibility
Computes per-group: estimated shared node_modules size, version conflicts count, compatibility_score (0-1)
Identifies monorepo candidates: groups of 3+ projects with >80% dep compatibility
Estimates disk savings: approximate node_modules size * (n_projects - 1) for each group
Adds 'environment_groups' to STFU JSON output with projects, conflicts, savings_estimate_mb
Tests: 2+ unit tests
userStories.notes: Analysis estimated 1-2 GB redundant node_modules across 22 npm projects.

userStories.title: AI Semantic Analysis Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU to use AI (via model-sw LiteLLM proxy) to semantically compare projects beyond structural similarity - detecting functional overlap, purpose similarity, and intelligent merge recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SemanticAnalyzer class to stfu_core.py
Uses existing ai_helper.py infrastructure (LiteLLM proxy at localhost:4000)
Batch-analyzes projects: sends README snippets + dep lists + file structure to AI for purpose classification
AI returns: {purpose, category, functional_overlap_candidates[], merge_recommendation, risk_assessment}
Compares project pairs semantically when structural score > 0.3: AI evaluates if they serve same/overlapping purpose
Graceful fallback: heuristic-only mode when AI unavailable (no errors, just lower confidence scores)
Caches AI results in ~/.cache/lfg/stfu_ai_cache.json with TTL (24h default)
Adds 'ai_analysis' section to STFU output when available
Tests: 1 unit test (mocked AI)
userStories.notes: model-sw LiteLLM proxy available at localhost:4000 with Azure OpenAI + Ollama backends.

userStories.title: Interactive HTML Report with Dashboard Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a rich STFU HTML report that renders in the LFG viewer with interactive tables, similarity heatmaps, cluster visualizations, and actionable recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: New lib/stfu_report.py generates .lfg_stfu.html from STFU JSON data
Report sections: Executive Summary, Duplicates (red), Relationships (purple), Clusters (green), Library Candidates (blue), Environment Groups (orange), AI Insights (if available)
Executive summary: project count, duplicate count, estimated savings, top 3 recommendations
Duplicates table: sortable by score, with file paths and 'View Diff' placeholder
Cluster cards: grouped projects with shared deps listed, color-coded by confidence
Library candidate cards: name, type, source projects, effort, priority badge
Environment groups: compatibility bars, conflict count, savings estimate
Uses existing LFG theme.css and ui.js (LFG.init, toast, tooltips, action buttons)
Integrates with dashboard.sh STFU main tab: dashboard loads stfu_report data
Navigation: LFG._postNav works for cross-module links
lfg stfu opens the report in viewer
userStories.notes: Must use existing LFG design system. Report should feel native to the LFG platform.

userStories.title: Merge Wizard and Coalescence Workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU to provide actionable merge/coalescence commands that I can execute from the UI - not just analysis but actual operations via the Action Bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: Add merge subcommands to stfu.sh: stfu merge-check <a> <b>, stfu merge-plan <a> <b>, stfu archive <project>
merge-check: runs full comparison (deps, structure, code patterns) between two projects, outputs compatibility report
merge-plan: generates step-by-step merge plan with commands (git remote add, rsync selective, dep merge, conflict list)
archive: moves project to ~/Developer/.archive/ with timestamp, removes from active scan
HTML report includes action buttons: 'Check Merge' (runs merge-check via LFG.exec), 'Archive' (runs archive via LFG.confirm)
Dashboard STFU tab duplicate rows have 'Merge Check' and 'Archive' action buttons
Toast feedback for all operations
Tests: 1 unit test for merge-check output format
userStories.notes: This makes STFU actionable, not just analytical. Uses Action Bridge from US-001 of v2.0.

userStories.title: CLI Dispatcher and Module Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STFU fully integrated into the LFG CLI with subcommands and the lfg dispatcher routing correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: lfg stfu (no args) runs full analysis and opens HTML report
lfg stfu --json outputs JSON to stdout
lfg stfu deps runs dependency analysis only
lfg stfu fingerprint runs file pattern analysis only
lfg stfu duplicates runs code duplicate detection only
lfg stfu libraries runs shared library candidate analysis only
lfg stfu envs runs environment consolidation analysis only
lfg stfu merge-check <a> <b> runs merge compatibility check
lfg stfu archive <project> archives a project
stfu.sh orchestrates stfu_core.py and stfu_report.py
state.sh tracks stfu module runs for APM
Updated help text in lfg --help

userStories.title: Polish, Testing, APM Sync, and Live Verification
userStories.description: Final integration: comprehensive testing against real ~/Developer portfolio, APM sync for all STFU operations, performance optimization for large portfolios.
acceptanceCriteria: Run full STFU analysis against ~/Developer (54 projects) - completes in <60 seconds
All JSON output validates against expected schema
HTML report renders correctly in LFG viewer
Dashboard STFU tab populates from live data
Side inspector panel shows top relationships
Action buttons (Merge Check, Archive) work via Action Bridge
state.sh fires APM notifications for stfu start/complete
All unit tests pass
Version noted in STFU report footer
CCEM APM shows all 10 STFU tasks as completed
userStories.notes: Live integration testing against real portfolio is the acceptance gate.
